example.value: "Tulog kot penes ajeh, eng ngha-pes deg," engrok amek nu menang eng.

translation.value: "Tolong ambil penyapu itu, saya mahu menyapu rumah," kata ibu kepada adik saya.
"Please get that broom, I want to sweep the house," mother said to my younger sibling.

example.value: "Amen hapan-pes begei ajeh, dij habok naiheg majeh-manen. Maleh iguna habesapu amen begei ajeh?" Wak Endoi kiurok nu ikenon menaleh.

translation.value: "Jika kamu menghayun penyapu tersebut dengan kuat, semua habuk akan terbang ke merata-rata. Apa gunanya kamu menyapu jika berbuat demikian?" Wak Endoi berkata kepada anak gadisnya.
"If you swing the broom hard, the dust will fly everywhere. What is the point of sweeping the floor if you do it like that?" Wak Endoi said to his daughter.

example-2.value: "Jeg mutu hek adeh nu majeh, eng ngha-pes sampah ku padeh," amek kiurok nu tenek eng.

translation-2.value: "Pindahkan motosikal kamu ke situ, saya mahu menyapu sampah (yang ada) di sini," ibu berkata kepada abang saya.
"Move your motorcycle over there, I want to sweep up the rabbish (which is) here," my mother said to my brother.

a person's reaction to a sound that he or she is familiar with
"Arok apak ya kijog!" engrok Bah Caca nu imenang sambel kijar ju ibilek nu luar deg bile kicengai engrok kerite de kikenal.
"Itu bapa sudah pulang!" Bah Caca berkata kepada adiknya sambil berlari dari biliknya ke luar rumah apabila mendengar bunyi kereta yang dikenalinya.
"That is father, he has just returned home!" Bah Caca said to his brother as he ran from the room to the outside of the house when he heard the sound of a car that he recognised.
